region_of_origin: Indian subcontinent; widely consumed in Singapore's Indian community at Deepavali, weddings, and temple prasad
food_subgroup: Warm semolina-based sweet pudding; ghee-roasted sooji/rava cooked with sugar syrup, cardamom, and optional saffron, cashews, and raisins

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Sooji Halwa / semolina halwa. Closest parent is semolina pudding. No exact URI available; null assigned per ontology honesty policy.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite food — sweet semolina-based pudding; mapped to FoodEx2 'Composite dishes' as closest defensible parent. No specific Sooji Halwa code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Sooji Halwa. A000E (Composite dishes) used as closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Sooji Halwa or semolina halwa as of 2026.

serving.description: 1 serving = 80g (standard open house portion served in a small bowl or on a banana leaf)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Sooji Halwa at a Deepavali open house or Indian festival is approximately 80g, typically presented in a small bowl or on a banana leaf.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Sooji Halwa / Semolina Halwa
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), Hyderabad; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (Semolina, cooked; Ghee; Sugar) for macro validation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods is the primary source for Sooji Halwa nutrient values. Values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component data for semolina, ghee, and sugar to confirm macro plausibility. ICMR-NIN is an approved primary regional source for Indian dishes per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Semolina-based Foods — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., 'International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021', American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Sooji Halwa exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of ~58 (medium) is estimated from semolina-based analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report semolina pasta and semolina porridge in the medium GI range (55–65). The semolina protein matrix slows starch digestion relative to refined wheat flour products. GL per 80g serving = (58 × 42g carbohydrate per 100g × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 19.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (30mg per 100g; ~24mg per 80g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Sooji Halwa is very low in sodium (~24mg per standard 80g serving) and poses no meaningful sodium burden for hypertensive patients; portion size remains the primary consideration given its caloric density from ghee and sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~58) with moderate-high glycemic load per serving (~19.5); high sugar content (28g per 100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sooji Halwa has a medium GI (~58) attributable to the semolina protein matrix slowing starch digestion, making it a relatively better choice than high-GI rice flour or glutinous rice-based festival sweets, but the glycemic load per 80g serving (~19.5) is moderate-high and T2DM patients should limit intake to a small portion and avoid combining with other high-carbohydrate festival foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (7g per 100g; ~5.6g per 80g serving) and cholesterol (25mg per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The ghee content contributes approximately 5.6g of saturated fat per 80g serving, which represents a meaningful proportion of the recommended daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular patients; individuals with CVD should treat Sooji Halwa as an occasional festival food and limit serving size to reduce saturated fat and caloric load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sooji Halwa (also known as Suji Halwa, Rava Halwa, or Semolina Halwa; Tamil: ரவா அல்வா; Hindi: सूजी का हलवा) is a warm, amber-coloured Indian sweet pudding made by dry-roasting fine semolina (sooji/rava) in ghee until fragrant and golden, then incorporating hot sugar syrup and cooking to a soft, glossy, semi-solid consistency. The dish is perfumed with cardamom and optionally saffron, and garnished with golden cashews and plump raisins. It is a staple at Deepavali open houses, Indian temple prasad offerings, and Indian weddings across Singapore and the broader Indian subcontinent. Nutritionally, Sooji Halwa provides 280 kcal per 100g, with 12g fat (predominantly saturated from ghee), 42g carbohydrate, 28g sugar, and 4g protein. Its medium glycemic index (~58) — lower than rice flour or glutinous rice-based sweets — is a genuine clinical advantage attributable to the semolina protein matrix slowing starch digestion. However, the glycemic load per standard 80g serving (~19.5) is moderate-high, and the saturated fat contribution from ghee (~5.6g per serving) is relevant for cardiovascular patients. For T2DM patients at a Deepavali function choosing among multiple sweets, Sooji Halwa is among the better GI options compared to Adhirasam or Nian Gao, though portion control remains essential. Sodium is very low (~30mg per 100g), making it non-concerning for hypertensive patients from a salt perspective.
common_user_queries: is sooji halwa good for diabetics

confidence_reason: ICMR-NIN is an approved primary source for Indian dishes and provides Sooji Halwa nutrient data. GI is an analogous estimate from semolina-based foods in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, as no direct GI measurement for Sooji Halwa exists; this limits confidence to medium. Macro values are plausible and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component data.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Sooji Halwa as a composite dish not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite preparation not reported in ICMR-NIN source. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/zero per ICMR-NIN and USDA ghee data — ghee from modern dairy processing contains negligible industrial trans fats). vitamin_c_mg reported as 0.0: Sooji Halwa contains no vitamin C-contributing ingredients; confirmed zero is consistent with ICMR-NIN and USDA component data. All core macros (protein_g 4.0, fat_g 12.0, carbohydrate_g 42.0) are non-zero and sourced from ICMR-NIN. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on semolina-based foods from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021; no direct GI measurement for Sooji Halwa exists in the approved GI database. Chinese local names (粗面粉甜糕 / 粗麵粉甜糕) are descriptive translations ('coarse flour sweet cake') used as the closest natural Chinese equivalent; no established loanword exists in Chinese for this dish.
